summary: Cross-chain mapping for the FEGtoken benchmark. FEG had parallel deployments on BSC and Ethereum and was attacked on both within a short window. The bridge nature of FEG comes from these mirrored ecosystems sharing the same migrator-role pattern, not from cross-chain message passing — which is why the attack played out as two independent same-chain events linked by their root cause.

contracts.note: Ethereum-side mirror of the same migrator-role pattern. Drained in a parallel campaign with the same V2+V4 chain.

entity_map.role: FEG governance/utility token. Hosts the `claimMigrator` transient-balance gate that the flash loan defeated.
FEGSwap router. Holds the user approvals; `swapToSwap` is the only-migrator-gated drain entry.
PancakeSwap V2 Router used as the flash-loan venue. Itself not vulnerable; correctly enforces the post-callback balance check.
Multiple users (and some smart-contract counterparties) who had previously approved FEGSwap to spend their MockToken balances for legitimate swaps.
Attacker EOA cluster — multiple sibling addresses across BSC and ETH.

asset_map.amount_drained: 0 (FEG itself was the flash-loan vehicle, not the drain target)
asset_map.notes: FEG was borrowed from the flash-loan pool to satisfy the migrator gate, then repaid intact. Net flow of FEG is zero — the drained value comes from MockToken approvals.
BUSD allowances were the largest single drain target on the BSC leg.
WBNB approvals also drained; secondary asset on BSC leg.
Smaller ETH-leg drain — same V2+V4 pattern executed against the ETH-side FEGSwap deployment.

bsc.vulnerable_param: from (3rd argument) — pulled via FEGSwap's prior approvals

ethereum.note: Same function signature on ETH leg.

message_map.message_id_field: n/a (no cross-chain messages — two parallel same-chain events)

invariants_for_fuzzer.description: Migrator role assignment must require sustained balance ownership across at least 1 confirmation block — transient flash-loaned balance must not satisfy the gate.

FEGSwap.swapToSwap must require an authorisation chain that ties the operation to the source-account holder, not just to the migrator role (e.g. signed permit, msg.sender == from, or governance proposal id).
A user's approve(FEGSwap, X) authorises FEGSwap to act on the user's own swap requests; it does not authorise arbitrary migrator-driven drains.
For any user u who has not signed a transaction in block B, MockToken.balanceOf(u) at end of block B >= MockToken.balanceOf(u) at start of block B.
FlashLoanProvider correctly enforces post-callback balance check (this invariant is preserved — the flash-loan provider itself is not the bug).
